# Next.js i18n Starter — LLM Context File
# https://llmstxt.org/ — machine-readable project reference for AI assistants
# Repository: https://github.com/S0vers/next-app-i18n-starter
# Live demo: https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app

> Next.js 16 App Router boilerplate with next-intl 4, locale-driven currency/date/time formatting, shadcn UI, RTL support, cookie-based theme SSR, and full SEO (hreflang, JSON-LD, sitemap).

---

## Quick answers for common tasks

### How do I add a new language?
1. Copy `dictionary/en.json` → `dictionary/xx.json` and translate all namespaces.
2. Add `"xx"` to `locales` in `src/i18n/routing.ts`.
3. Add `{ currency, timeZone }` to `localeRegionalDefaults` in `src/i18n/regional.ts`.
4. Add label to `languageLabels` in `src/components/LanguageSwitcher.tsx`.
5. Add BCP 47 mapping to `openGraphLocales` in `src/lib/site.ts` (e.g. `fr: "fr_FR"`).
6. Run `bun run build` — TypeScript picks up new keys via `global.d.ts` (typed from `en.json`).

### How does currency/timezone formatting work?
Locale drives everything. No user override dropdowns or cookies.
- `src/i18n/request.ts` → `getRequestConfig` sets `timeZone`, `now`, `formats` per locale.
- `src/i18n/regional.ts` → `localeRegionalDefaults` maps locale → currency + IANA time zone.
- Client: `useFormatter()`, `useTimeZone()`, `useNow()` from `next-intl`.
- Demo UI: `src/components/LocalizationTab.tsx` — switch language in header to see changes.

### How do I add a new page?
1. Create `src/app/[locale]/your-page/page.tsx` (Server Component).
2. Call `setRequestLocale(locale)` at top of page (enables static rendering).
3. Use `getTranslations({ locale, namespace: "YourNamespace" })` for server text.
4. Use `useTranslations("YourNamespace")` in client components.
5. Add keys to all `dictionary/*.json` files.
6. Link with `Link` from `@/i18n/navigation` (not `next/link` directly).

### How do I navigate between locales?
Always use `@/i18n/navigation` wrappers — they respect `localePrefix: as-needed`:
```tsx
import { Link, useRouter, usePathname } from "@/i18n/navigation";
router.replace(pathname, { locale: "ar" });
```

### How do I update SEO metadata?
Edit `dictionary/{locale}/Metadata` in all 5 JSON files (`title`, `description`, `keywords`). Layout `generateMetadata` in `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` reads this namespace via `getTranslations({ locale, namespace: "Metadata" })`. Rebuild after changes.

### How do I add SEO to a new page?
1. Add `metaTitle` / `metaDescription` keys to a new namespace in all dictionary files.
2. Export `generateMetadata` from the page using `getTranslations` + `getPathname({ locale, href: "/your-path" })` for canonical.
3. Add a sitemap entry in `src/app/sitemap.ts` with `alternates.languages` per locale.
4. Optionally add page-specific JSON-LD in the page Server Component.

### How are hreflang URLs built?
Never hardcode `/en` or `/ar`. Use `getPathname({ locale, href: "/" })` from `@/i18n/navigation`, then `new URL(pathname, siteConfig.url)`. With `localePrefix: as-needed`, English is `/` (no prefix), others are `/ar`, `/zh`, etc.

### What is proxy.ts vs middleware?
Next.js 16 renamed middleware to proxy. This project uses `src/proxy.ts` with `createMiddleware` from `next-intl/middleware`. Same role: locale detection, redirects, cookie handling.

---

## Stack and versions

| Package | Version | Role |
|---------|---------|------|
| next | 16.2.x | App Router, Turbopack (default in dev), Metadata API |
| react / react-dom | 19.2.x | Server Components, no `<script>` in client trees |
| next-intl | 4.13.x | i18n routing, ICU messages, formatters |
| tailwindcss | 4.3.x | Styling via `@tailwindcss/postcss` |
| motion | 12.x | Animations (`motion/react`, not framer-motion) |
| lucide-react | 1.22.x | Icons |
| @vercel/analytics | 1.6.x | Analytics in root layout |
| @vercel/speed-insights | 1.3.x | Performance insights |

Package manager: **bun** (preferred). pnpm also works.

---

---

## Environment variables

| Variable | Required | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL | No (has default) | Production URL for metadataBase, sitemap, canonical, OG. Default: `https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app` |

Copy `.env.example` → `.env.local`. Never commit secrets.

---

## Architecture

```
Request
  → src/proxy.ts (next-intl middleware)
  → src/i18n/routing.ts (locales, localePrefix: as-needed)
  → src/i18n/request.ts (getRequestConfig)
       ├── locale from URL / cookie / Accept-Language
       ├── timeZone from regional.ts
       ├── formats (currency, dateTime, compact) from regional.ts
       └── messages from dictionary/{locale}.json
  → src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx
       ├── generateMetadata (SEO)
       ├── theme cookie → className on <html>
       └── NextIntlClientProvider (messages, timeZone, now)
  → page.tsx + client components
```

### URL routing (`localePrefix: as-needed`)

| Locale | URL path | HTML dir |
|--------|----------|----------|
| en (default) | `/` | ltr |
| ar | `/ar` | rtl |
| zh | `/zh` | ltr |
| es | `/es` | ltr |
| ja | `/ja` | ltr |

`localeDetection: true` — browser language used on first visit.

---

## File reference

### i18n core
- `src/proxy.ts` — next-intl middleware export + matcher config
- `src/i18n/routing.ts` — `defineRouting({ locales, defaultLocale, localePrefix })`
- `src/i18n/request.ts` — `getRequestConfig` plugin entry (wired in `next.config.ts`)
- `src/i18n/regional.ts` — `localeRegionalDefaults`, `resolveCurrency`, `resolveTimeZone`, `createRegionalFormats`
- `src/i18n/navigation.ts` — `createNavigation(routing)` → `Link`, `redirect`, `useRouter`, `usePathname`, `getPathname`
- `next.config.ts` — `createNextIntlPlugin("./src/i18n/request.ts")`

### App routes
- `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` — root layout, `generateMetadata`, `generateStaticParams`, theme SSR
- `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` — home page, JSON-LD (WebSite + Person schemas)
- `src/app/[locale]/[...rest]/page.tsx` — catch-all 404
- `src/app/[locale]/not-found.tsx` — localized not-found
- `src/app/sitemap.ts` — sitemap with `alternates.languages` via `getPathname`
- `src/app/robots.ts` — dynamic robots.txt
- `src/app/error.tsx` — global error boundary

### Components
- `src/components/pages/HomeIndex.tsx` — main landing page (tabs: install, OmitRTL, contribute, localization)
- `src/components/LocalizationTab.tsx` — locale formatting demo (read-only, no copy buttons)
- `src/components/LanguageSwitcher.tsx` — `router.replace(pathname, { locale })`
- `src/components/ModeToggle.tsx` — light/dark toggle via `useTheme()`
- `src/components/theme-provider.tsx` — client theme context, cookie + localStorage sync
- `src/components/OmmitRlt.tsx` — force LTR in RTL locales (note: filename typo is intentional/historical)
- `src/components/ui/*` — shadcn/ui primitives (button, card, tabs, dropdown-menu, select)

### Lib
- `src/lib/site.ts` — `siteConfig` (name, url, author, github), `openGraphLocales`
- `src/lib/theme.ts` — `THEME_STORAGE_KEY`, `resolveSSRTheme`, `setThemeCookie`
- `src/lib/utils.ts` — `cn()` helper (tailwind-merge + clsx)
- `global.d.ts` — `IntlMessages` typed from `dictionary/en.json`

### Translations
- `dictionary/en.json` — source of truth for TypeScript message keys
- `dictionary/ar.json`, `zh.json`, `es.json`, `ja.json` — must mirror en.json structure

### Translation namespaces (en.json)
- `Index` — landing page UI (hero, tabs, installation steps, contribute steps, errors)
- `Footer` — copyright, links
- `Metadata` — SEO title, description, keywords (used in `generateMetadata`)
- `Localization` — localization tab labels and ICU format examples

### ICU message examples in Localization namespace
```json
"priceMessage": "This product costs {price, number, currency}"
"usersCount": "{count, number, compact} users"
```
Requires matching `formats` in `createRegionalFormats()`.

---

## Regional defaults

| Locale | Language | Currency | Time zone | RTL |
|--------|----------|----------|-----------|-----|
| en | English | USD | America/New_York | No |
| ar | Arabic | SAR | Asia/Riyadh | Yes |
| zh | Chinese | CNY | Asia/Shanghai | No |
| es | Spanish | EUR | Europe/Madrid | No |
| ja | Japanese | JPY | Asia/Tokyo | No |

Format names available via `useFormatter()`:
- `format.number(value, "price")` — currency with 2 decimal places
- `format.number(value, "compact")` — compact notation (e.g. 12.8K)
- `format.dateTime(date, "long")` — full date + time in locale time zone
- `format.dateTime(date, "short")` — medium date + short time
- `format.relativeTime(from, to)` — relative time (e.g. "2 hours ago")

---

## SEO — complete implementation reference

This section documents every SEO mechanism in the project. Read this before modifying metadata, sitemap, structured data, or social tags.

### SEO file map

| File | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `src/lib/site.ts` | `siteConfig` (name, url, author, github), `openGraphLocales` BCP 47 map |
| `src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx` | `generateMetadata()` → all `<head>` meta/link tags per locale |
| `src/app/[locale]/page.tsx` | JSON-LD `WebSite` + `Person` schemas (home page only) |
| `src/app/sitemap.ts` | Generates `/sitemap.xml` with hreflang alternates |
| `src/app/robots.ts` | Generates `/robots.txt` pointing to sitemap |
| `dictionary/en.json` → `Metadata` | Source of truth for SEO text keys (typed via global.d.ts) |
| `dictionary/ar.json` etc. | Translated Metadata per locale |
| `public/og-image.png` | 1200×630 Open Graph / Twitter preview image |
| `public/google52d37058772b10e6.html` | Alternate Google Search Console verification file |
| `.env.example` | Documents `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` |

### Central config: src/lib/site.ts

```ts
export const siteConfig = {
  name: "Next.js i18n Starter",
  url: process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL ?? "https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app",
  github: "https://github.com/S0vers/next-app-i18n-starter",
  author: {
    name: "Sovers Tonmoy Pandey",
    alias: "S0vers",
    url: "https://s0vers.com",
    twitter: "@s0ver5",
    github: "https://github.com/S0vers",
  },
};

export const openGraphLocales = {
  en: "en_US", ar: "ar_SA", zh: "zh_CN", es: "es_ES", ja: "ja_JP",
};
```

ALL absolute URLs in SEO output derive from `siteConfig.url`. Set `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL` in production.

### generateMetadata — src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx

Async function called by Next.js Metadata API per `[locale]` segment.

**Helper:**
```ts
async function getLocaleUrl(locale: string) {
  const pathname = await getPathname({ locale, href: "/" });
  return new URL(pathname, siteConfig.url).toString();
}
```

**Full return object and HTML output:**

| Field | HTML output | Notes |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| `metadataBase: new URL(siteConfig.url)` | (base for relative URLs) | Resolves `/og-image.png` to absolute URL |
| `title` | `<title>` | From `dictionary/{locale}/Metadata.title` |
| `description` | `<meta name="description" content="...">` | Localized per locale |
| `keywords` | `<meta name="keywords" content="...">` | Localized per locale |
| `authors: [{ name, url }]` | `<meta name="author" content="...">` | Sovers Tonmoy Pandey, https://s0vers.com |
| `creator` | `<meta name="creator" content="@s0ver5">` | Twitter handle |
| `applicationName` | `<meta name="application-name">` | siteConfig.name |
| `alternates.canonical` | `<link rel="canonical" href="...">` | Locale-specific absolute URL |
| `alternates.languages` | `<link rel="alternate" hreflang="xx" href="...">` × 5 | All locales linked from every locale page |
| `openGraph.title` | `<meta property="og:title">` | Same as title |
| `openGraph.description` | `<meta property="og:description">` | Same as description |
| `openGraph.url` | `<meta property="og:url">` | Locale canonical (NOT bare domain) |
| `openGraph.siteName` | `<meta property="og:site_name">` | siteConfig.name |
| `openGraph.images` | `<meta property="og:image">` + width/height/alt | `/og-image.png`, 1200×630 |
| `openGraph.locale` | `<meta property="og:locale">` | BCP 47: en_US, ar_SA, etc. |
| `openGraph.type` | `<meta property="og:type">` | `website` |
| `twitter.card` | `<meta name="twitter:card">` | `summary_large_image` |
| `twitter.title` | `<meta name="twitter:title">` | Localized |
| `twitter.description` | `<meta name="twitter:description">` | Localized |
| `twitter.images` | `<meta name="twitter:image">` | `/og-image.png` |
| `twitter.creator` | `<meta name="twitter:creator">` | `@s0ver5` |
| `robots.index/follow` | `<meta name="robots" content="index, follow">` | Allow crawling |
| `robots.googleBot` | `<meta name="googlebot" ...>` | max-video-preview: -1, max-image-preview: large, max-snippet: -1 |
| `other["google-site-verification"]` | `<meta name="google-site-verification">` | Search Console token |

### hreflang URL table (localePrefix: as-needed)

| Locale | getPathname({ locale, href: "/" }) | Example canonical |
|--------|-----------------------------------|-------------------|
| en | `/` | https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app/ |
| ar | `/ar` | https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app/ar |
| zh | `/zh` | https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app/zh |
| es | `/es` | https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app/es |
| ja | `/ja` | https://next-app-i18n-starter.vercel.app/ja |

`alternates.languages` in generateMetadata builds all 5 hreflang links on every locale's page.

### Translation-driven metadata — dictionary Metadata namespace

```json
"Metadata": {
  "title": "Next.js 16 i18n Starter - Multilingual Template by S0vers",
  "description": "Next.js 16 internationalization starter with next-intl 4...",
  "keywords": "Next.js 16, next-intl, i18n, internationalization..."
}
```

Present in: en.json, ar.json, zh.json, es.json, ja.json. Keys are type-checked via global.d.ts (IntlMessages = typeof en).

### HTML semantics in layout

```tsx
<html lang={locale} dir={isArabic ? "rtl" : "ltr"} className={ssrTheme}>
```

- `lang` — BCP 47 language tag for crawlers and assistive tech
- `dir` — `rtl` for Arabic only; `ltr` for all others
- `generateStaticParams()` — pre-renders all 5 locale variants at build

Additional `<head>` elements in layout (not via Metadata API):
- `<link rel="icon" href="/favicon.ico">`
- `<meta name="theme-color" content="#000000">`
- Google Fonts preconnect links

### JSON-LD — src/app/[locale]/page.tsx

Two `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks in the home page Server Component.

**XSS-safe serialization (required by Next.js docs):**
```ts
function serializeJsonLd(data: Record<string, unknown>) {
  return JSON.stringify(data).replace(/</g, "\\u003c");
}
```

**Schema 1 — WebSite:**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "WebSite",
  "name": "Next.js i18n Starter",
  "description": "<from Metadata.description, localized>",
  "url": "<siteConfig.url>",
  "inLanguage": "<current locale, e.g. ja>",
  "author": { "@type": "Person", "name": "Sovers Tonmoy Pandey", "url": "https://s0vers.com" }
}
```

**Schema 2 — Person (author):**
```json
{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "Person",
  "name": "Sovers Tonmoy Pandey",
  "alternateName": "S0vers",
  "url": "https://s0vers.com",
  "sameAs": [
    "https://github.com/S0vers",
    "https://twitter.com/s0ver5",
    "https://github.com/S0vers/next-app-i18n-starter"
  ]
}
```

IMPORTANT: JSON-LD uses server-component `<script>` tags directly — NOT `next/script`, NOT client components. React 19 blocks scripts in client component trees.

Validate at: https://search.google.com/test/rich-results

### Sitemap — src/app/sitemap.ts

Route: `GET /sitemap.xml` (auto-generated by Next.js App Router)

```ts
export default async function sitemap(): Promise<MetadataRoute.Sitemap> {
  const languages = Object.fromEntries(
    await Promise.all(
      routing.locales.map(async (locale) => [
        locale,
        new URL(await getPathname({ locale, href: "/" }), siteConfig.url).toString(),
      ]),
    ),
  );

  return [{
    url: siteConfig.url,
    lastModified: new Date(),
    changeFrequency: "monthly",
    priority: 1,
    alternates: { languages },
  }];
}
```

**Design:** ONE url entry with `alternates.languages` — NOT separate entries per locale. Matches next-intl recommended pattern. Prevents duplicate URL rows.

When adding pages, add more objects to the return array, each with its own `alternates.languages` built via `getPathname({ locale, href: "/page-path" })`.

### Robots — src/app/robots.ts

Route: `GET /robots.txt`

```ts
export default function robots(): MetadataRoute.Robots {
  return {
    rules: { userAgent: "*", allow: "/" },
    sitemap: `${siteConfig.url}/sitemap.xml`,
  };
}
```

Output:
```
User-Agent: *
Allow: /
Sitemap: https://your-domain.com/sitemap.xml
```

Replaces the old static `src/app/robots.txt` file (deleted).

### Open Graph image

- Path: `public/og-image.png`
- Dimensions: 1200×630 (1.91:1)
- Referenced as relative `/og-image.png` in metadata
- Resolved to absolute URL via `metadataBase`
- Used by both `openGraph.images` and `twitter.images`
- `alt` text = localized Metadata.title

### Google Search Console

Two verification methods present:
1. Meta tag via `other["google-site-verification"]` in generateMetadata
2. Static HTML file at `public/google52d37058772b10e6.html`

Replace verification token when deploying to a new domain.

### Adding SEO to a new page — step by step

Example: `/about` page

1. Create `src/app/[locale]/about/page.tsx`
2. Add to all dictionary files:
   ```json
   "About": { "metaTitle": "...", "metaDescription": "..." }
   ```
3. Page-level generateMetadata:
   ```ts
   const pathname = await getPathname({ locale, href: "/about" });
   const canonical = new URL(pathname, siteConfig.url).toString();
   return { title: t("metaTitle"), description: t("metaDescription"), alternates: { canonical } };
   ```
4. Extend sitemap.ts with new entry + alternates.languages for `/about`
5. Optional: add JSON-LD script block in the page component

### SEO pitfalls (project-specific)

- DO NOT hardcode `/en` — English uses `/` with as-needed prefix
- DO NOT duplicate locale URL rows in sitemap when using alternates.languages
- DO NOT put JSON-LD in client components or use next/script
- DO NOT hardcode domain — use siteConfig.url
- DO NOT forget to translate Metadata namespace when adding a locale
- DO NOT use next/link for canonical URL construction — use getPathname

### SEO verification commands

```bash
curl -s http://localhost:3000/sitemap.xml
curl -s http://localhost:3000/robots.txt
curl -s http://localhost:3000/ar | grep -E 'lang=|canonical|hreflang|og:|twitter:'
curl -s http://localhost:3000 | grep 'application/ld+json'
```

### Production SEO checklist

- Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL to production domain
- Replace public/og-image.png
- Update google-site-verification token
- Translate Metadata in all dictionary files
- Submit sitemap.xml in Google Search Console
- Test hreflang, JSON-LD, OG previews

### External SEO references

- Next.js Metadata API: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/api-reference/functions/generate-metadata
- Next.js JSON-LD guide: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/json-ld
- next-intl sitemap pattern: https://next-intl.dev/docs/environments/actions-metadata-route-handlers#sitemap
- Google hreflang docs: https://developers.google.com/search/docs/specialty/international/localized-versions

---

## Theme system

React 19 blocks `<script>` tags in component trees. Theme uses cookie SSR instead:

1. Server (`layout.tsx`): read `theme` cookie → `resolveSSRTheme()` → `className` on `<html>`
2. Client (`theme-provider.tsx`): `useSyncExternalStore` for system preference, syncs cookie + localStorage on change
3. `ModeToggle.tsx`: toggles between `light` / `dark` (not system)

Cookie name: `theme` (see `src/lib/theme.ts`).

---

## RTL and OmitRTL

Arabic (`ar`) sets `dir="rtl"` on `<html>`. Code blocks, terminal commands, and formatted numbers should stay LTR:

```tsx
import OmitRTL from "@/components/OmmitRlt";
<OmitRTL omitRTL>{children}</OmitRTL>
```

NPM package also available: `react-omit-rtl`.

---

## Scripts

```bash
bun install    # Install dependencies
bun dev        # Dev server (Turbopack)
bun build      # Production build + typecheck
bun start      # Production server
bun lint       # ESLint (eslint-config-next flat config)
```

---

## Conventions and pitfalls

DO:
- Use `Link`, `useRouter`, `usePathname` from `@/i18n/navigation`
- Use `setRequestLocale(locale)` in every `[locale]` page/layout for static rendering
- Add translation keys to ALL dictionary files when adding features
- Use `getPathname` for SEO URLs (respects `as-needed` prefix)
- Prefer Server Components; add `"use client"` only when needed

DON'T:
- Use `next/link` or `next/navigation` directly for locale-aware routing
- Add `<Script>` or inline `<script>` in client components (React 19 error)
- Hardcode site URL — use `siteConfig.url` or `NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL`
- Duplicate locale arrays — import `routing.locales` from `src/i18n/routing.ts`
- Hardcode hreflang URLs like `/en` — English canonical is `/` with as-needed routing
- Duplicate per-locale rows in sitemap.ts when alternates.languages is used
- Put JSON-LD in client components — only Server Components (see page.tsx)
- Touch `.env` in automation — use `.env.example` as reference only

---

## Type safety

`global.d.ts` imports `dictionary/en.json` as `IntlMessages`. All `useTranslations("Namespace")` and `t("key")` calls are type-checked against en.json structure. When adding keys, update en.json first.

---

## External documentation

- next-intl: https://next-intl.dev
- Next.js App Router: https://nextjs.org/docs/app
- Next.js Metadata API: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/building-your-application/optimizing/metadata
- Next.js JSON-LD: https://nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/json-ld
- shadcn/ui: https://ui.shadcn.com
